Title: The AVR4 effector is involved in cercosporin biosynthesis and likely affects the virulence of Cercospora cf. flagellaris on soybean

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fungal virulence factor; contributes to host necrosis/chlorosis and disease development on soybean
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AVR4 positively affects cercosporin biosynthesis and contributes to virulence; loss or dsRNA silencing of Avr4 drastically reduces cercosporin production and lowers virulence
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted gene disruption; dsRNA-mediated gene silencing; qRT-PCR of Avr4, CTB1 and CTB8; cercosporin quantification by absorbance and HPLC; detached soybean leaf virulence assay; fungal biomass qPCR; chitinase sensitivity assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in wild type grown in culture; reduced by 78% after Avr4 dsRNA treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in wild type; absent in avr4 mutants; reduced after Avr4 dsRNA treatment
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: apoplastic effector likely secreted; exact localization in this paper NA
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AVR4 effector; chitin-binding protein with peritrophin-A domain (pfam01607); cysteine-rich
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light/dark culture conditions; chitinase exposure; Avr4-specific dsRNA treatment
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: avr4 mutants grew faster on PDA but were more chitinase-sensitive and less virulent on soybean
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Disruption of Avr4 reduced cercosporin production to about 10% on PDA and 3-7% in liquid media relative to wild type. 2. Avr4 disruption reduced CTB1 and CTB8 expression by about two- to three-fold. 3. avr4 mutants were more sensitive to chitinase and caused smaller lesions with about 50% lower fungal biomass in soybean leaves. 4. Avr4 dsRNA suppressed Avr4 expression by 78%, reduced cercosporin by 88%, and also lowered CTB1 and CTB8 expression, independently confirming AVR4 involvement in cercosporin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary core gene of the paper. Although mutants contained multiple HYG copies, independent dsRNA silencing reproduced the low-cercosporin phenotype, supporting a direct AVR4 role. AVR4 is not a pathway enzyme or TF; it is a fungal effector/chitin-binding virulence protein affecting pathway gene expression.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avr4 is a potential disease-control target for RNA-based strategies to suppress cercosporin production and reduce Cercospora leaf blight virulence.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fungal mycelium, culture medium-associated pigment, and infected soybean leaves

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fungal virulence factor; contributes to host tissue damage and disease development
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: polyketide synthase step required for cercosporin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: lower CTB1 expression is associated with strongly reduced cercosporin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression analysis in avr4 disruption mutants and Avr4-dsRNA-treated wild type, coupled with cercosporin quantification by absorbance/HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expression reduced in avr4 mutants under both light and dark conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expression reduced in avr4 mutants and in Avr4-dsRNA-treated cultures
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light versus dark culture conditions; Avr4-targeting dsRNA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CTB1 expression was significantly reduced about two- to three-fold in avr4 mutants. 2. CTB1 expression was also reduced by about 75% after Avr4 dsRNA treatment. 3. Reduced CTB1 expression correlated with major loss of cercosporin production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CTB1 was measured as a pathway readout, not directly functionally manipulated in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fungal mycelium and infected soybean leaves

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: enzymatic step required for cercosporin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: lower CTB8 expression is associated with strongly reduced cercosporin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression analysis in avr4 disruption mutants and Avr4-dsRNA-treated wild type, coupled with cercosporin quantification by HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expression reduced in avr4 mutants under both light and dark conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expression reduced in avr4 mutants and in Avr4-dsRNA-treated cultures
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light versus dark culture conditions; Avr4-targeting dsRNA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CTB8 expression was significantly reduced about two- to three-fold in avr4 mutants. 2. CTB8 expression was also reduced by about 58% after Avr4 dsRNA treatment. 3. Reduced CTB8 expression correlated with major loss of cercosporin production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CTB8 was measured as a pathway readout, not directly functionally manipulated in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fungal mycelium and infected soybean leaves
